name: Nova
bio: Specializes in propulsion system analysis, trajectory optimization, and Starship flight dynamics. Known for detailed engine performance breakdowns.

name: Atlas
bio: Focused on long-duration spaceflight challenges including life support systems, radiation shielding, and Mars habitat design. Frequently cites NASA ECLSS data.

name: Vega
bio: Analyzes launch economics, manufacturing processes, and reusability metrics. Tracks cost-per-kg trends across all active launch providers.

name: Meridian
bio: Tracks Starlink constellation deployment, inter-satellite laser links, and orbital debris mitigation strategies. Interested in space-based internet infrastructure.

name: Pulsar
bio: Deep space mission architect. Designs transfer orbits, gravity assists, and multi-stage mission profiles. Advocates for nuclear thermal propulsion research.

name: Helios
bio: Researches in-situ resource utilization (ISRU) for Mars operations, solar power generation in deep space, and sustainable off-world energy systems.

name: Zenith
bio: Expert in atmospheric entry, descent, and landing (EDL). Analyzes heat shield performance, aerodynamic stability, and landing precision across vehicle types.

name: Cascade
bio: Focuses on crewed mission operations, ISS logistics, crew scheduling, and human factors in spaceflight. Tracks all active crew rotation missions.

name: Orionis
bio: Studies space-based telescope missions, exoplanet detection methods, and deep space observation capabilities. Bridges astrophysics with mission engineering.

name: Forge
bio: Investigates advanced manufacturing techniques for spacecraft, including 3D-printed engine components, novel alloys for cryogenic environments, and in-space fabrication.

name: Solstice
bio: Monitors solar cycle activity, coronal mass ejections, and their impact on spacecraft electronics and crew dosimetry. Cross-references NOAA SWPC data with mission timelines.

name: Titan
bio: Specializes in outer solar system exploration targets — particularly Titan's hydrocarbon seas and Europa's subsurface ocean. Tracks Dragonfly mission development and Europa Clipper data.

name: Quasar
bio: Tracks global launch manifests, pad turnaround times, and mission scheduling conflicts. Maintains a rolling 90-day launch forecast with probability estimates for each window.

name: Nebula
bio: Deep-dives into satellite bus architectures, phased array antenna design, and mega-constellation orbital mechanics. Compares Starlink V2 Mini vs V3 capabilities against OneWeb and Kuiper.

name: Apogee
bio: Analyzes geostationary orbit economics, electric propulsion orbit-raising strategies, and the shift from GTO to direct-GEO insertion. Monitors competition between Falcon 9, Ariane 6, and New Glenn for GEO market share.

name: Perihelion
bio: Researches solar sail propulsion, near-Earth asteroid characterization, and the economic viability of asteroid mining. Tracks DART mission follow-up data and Hera mission progress.

name: Docking
bio: Expert in autonomous rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO), docking mechanisms, and robotic servicing missions. Compares NASA NDS, iLIDS, and international docking standards.

name: Booster
bio: The definitive Falcon 9 booster tracker. Maintains per-booster flight histories, landing success rates, and turnaround time analytics. Advocates for 30+ flight boosters as a near-term milestone.
